name: Christmas Stollen from Brigitte Sealing
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix and sieve together the flour and baking powder onto a pastry board or cool slab.
2. Make a well in the center and pour in the sugar, vanilla, rum, lemon juice, and eggs.
3. Draw in some flour from the sides of the well to mix with these ingredients and form a thick paste.
4. Add the cold butter, cut into small pieces, the finely chopped beef drippings, cottage cheese, currants, sultanas, nuts, and candied peel.
5. Cover the fruits with more of the flour, then work all of it together quickly with your hands into a firm, smooth paste. If it sticks, add a little more flour.
6. Form the mix into a long, oval shape, and then fold it over lengthwise to give it the traditional "stollen" shape.
7. Line a baking dish with greased waxed paper and place the stollen on this.
8. Preheat the oven for 5 minutes at 500°F. Then bake at a moderately hot temperature (375°F) for 50-60 minutes.
9. As soon as the stollen comes out of the oven, brush with melted butter and dust thickly with powdered sugar.
